notableMoments: The 2026 World Cup marks Curacao's first ever appearance at a World Cup, a historic milestone for the island nation of approximately 150,000 people
Qualified through the CONCACAF pathway, becoming one of the smallest nations ever to reach the World Cup finals
Many of Curacao's players were born in the Netherlands to parents of Curacao heritage, leveraging the historical ties between the island and the Dutch football system
Curacao's football association was founded in 1921, making their 2026 World Cup qualification the culmination of over a century of football development
Previously competed as the Netherlands Antilles until 2010, when the country was dissolved and Curacao became a constituent country of the Kingdom of the Netherlands
The Bacuna brothers (Juninho and Leandro) and Vurnon Anita represent the highest level of talent ever assembled for a Curacao squad, with Premier League and Bundesliga experience
Cuco Martina's famous long-range goal against Arsenal while at Southampton remains one of the most iconic moments by a Curacao international in European football
The 2026 World Cup squad features players from leagues across Europe including the Eredivisie, Scottish Premiership, Belgian Pro League, and MLS, showcasing the diaspora's contribution
